title: eurybia gunniana var. phlogopappa
intro: Eurybia gunniana var. phlogopappa, also known as the New Zealand flax, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its tall stalks and red or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Eurybia gunniana var. phlogopappa include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and even exhibiting antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
